content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the bead materialization feature on branch feat/9ni43-materialize-beads: new `milestone export-beads` CLI subcommand (src/cli/milestone.rs), core `materialize_beads` service in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, and adapter extensions for ParentChild dependencies (src/adapters/br_process.rs) and bead comments (src/adapters/br_models.rs). `cargo check` passes; `cargo clippy` produces only pre-existing warnings. Idempotency, concurrency protection (attempt file + Flock + liveness check), and dependency edges are wired correctly. Two real correctness gaps worth addressing: (1) the CLI handler leaks the attempt file on error paths, relying on the liveness check to eventually unstick state, which is fragile if users expect clean retries; (2) materialize_beads identifies workstream epics only by milestone_scope_label + title + type, which collides on re-run when the plan has two workstreams that happen to share a title — a case bundle validation intentionally permits (see plan_md_disambiguates_duplicate_workstream_names). Everything else I examined (proposal-id canonicalization, dependency ensure logic, `br create` output parsing with fallback to list_matching_beads_by_title, the custom BeadDetail Deserialize handling single-vs-array responses, the shift to --labels=csv in br_process) is sound.

## Amendments

1. In src/cli/milestone.rs around lines 383-500 (handle_export_beads), the attempt file reserved via reserve_bead_export_attempt is only cleared on the happy path via clear_bead_export_attempt. If materialize_beads or record_beads_exported_event returns Err (propagated via `?`), the attempt file and its `.lock` are left on disk with the current PID recorded. Recovery then depends on the next invocation's liveness check noticing the PID no longer matches a live process, which works for a hard crash but leaves stale state after ordinary command failures (e.g. br unavailable mid-run) that the user would reasonably expect to be retryable immediately. Please convert BeadExportAttemptGuard into a real RAII guard (Drop impl that calls clear_bead_export_attempt unless an explicit `.commit()` was invoked), or wrap the materialize/record calls so that clear_bead_export_attempt is called on both Ok and Err, then only the commit-on-success path is skipped on failure. This keeps error-path behavior consistent with the documented idempotency contract and avoids user confusion about why a retry reports an in-progress export.
   Rationale: Relying on the process-liveness sweep to clean up after recoverable error paths is fragile and leaks implementation detail to users doing normal retries.
2. In src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs around lines 1597-1614 (the workstream-epic creation loop inside materialize_beads), workstream epics are matched to existing beads only by (milestone_scope_label, type=epic, title) via existing_by_title. Task beads use canonical_export_proposal_id to add a per-proposal `proposal:<id>` label that distinguishes them, but workstream epics have no analogous disambiguator. bundle.validate() intentionally permits duplicate workstream names (see contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs tests plan_md_disambiguates_duplicate_workstream_names and the pub(crate) export of normalize_bead_reference / canonicalize_bead_reference). As a result, a plan with two workstreams sharing a title will either (a) create two distinct beads on the first run that both match the same (title, scope) key on re-run, causing find_existing_materialized_bead to fail with `active_eligible_indices.len() > 1`, or (b) collapse the second workstream into the first on first run, silently merging their tasks under the wrong parent. Please give workstream epics the same treatment as task beads: canonicalize the workstream reference (title + positional index within the bundle) into a stable workstream-scoped label such as `workstream:<canonical-id>`, attach it on creation, and match on it in find_existing_materialized_bead. The proposal_id_map keying can then stay title-based for bead dependencies that reference the workstream by name, but re-run identity should not depend on titles alone.
   Rationale: Bundle validation explicitly allows duplicate workstream titles, so materialization must not rely on title uniqueness for idempotent re-runs.
